claims.claim: In a prototype validation on a single consumer-grade GPU node (NVIDIA aarch64, Linux 6.17), we establish that a random axis-label baseline yields 0.130 accuracy on 100 generated items while an oracle solver achieves 1.000, confirming that items are internally solvable and far from trivial.

claims.claim: A smoke evaluation of Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Q4_K_M) on a balanced 24-item subset produces an overall accuracy of 0.708 but reveals pronounced axis-level separation: perfect scores on state tracking (6/6) and causal intervention (6/6), near-perfect belief tracking (5/6), and complete failure on program execution (0/6).
